title: Fundable Projects
description: Seed statements for the fundable-project explorer, grouped by cause area and accompanied by category-level notes about why each cluster exists.
notes: These are entry points for the fundable-project explorer rather than mechanically generated ideology axes.
Each statement is phrased as something a real person might sign.

groups.title: Finding common ground / depolarization
notes: High-priority cluster because it most directly matches Commonality's thesis.

statements.text: I am interested in furthering the cause of helping people find common ground across political divides.
I am interested in furthering the cause of content that explains one side's views to the other side charitably and accurately.
I am interested in furthering the cause of identifying where political opponents actually agree.
I am interested in furthering the cause of reducing tribal polarization in public discourse.
I am interested in furthering the cause of journalism that informs rather than inflames.

implicationNotes: All of these should imply the anti-tribalism meta statement.
All of these also connect to the noninflammatory content-funding seed statements.

groups.title: Government accountability and political reform
notes: Cross-cutting cause area with authentic left-right appeal.

statements.text: I am interested in furthering the cause of exposing government corruption and waste.
I am interested in furthering the cause of making government spending transparent and auditable.
I am interested in furthering the cause of congressional term limits.
I am interested in furthering the cause of getting money out of politics.
I am interested in furthering the cause of ending the revolving door between government and lobbying.
I am interested in furthering the cause of better voting systems and electoral integrity.
I am interested in furthering the cause of breaking up regulatory capture by industry.

implicationNotes: All of these should imply "I care about government accountability and transparency".
Term limits, money in politics, and revolving door should connect to hidden-majority commonality statements.

groups.title: Civil liberties / free speech / digital rights

statements.text: I am interested in furthering the cause of free speech, especially unpopular speech.
I am interested in furthering the cause of censorship-resistant publishing infrastructure.
I am interested in furthering the cause of protecting people from government surveillance.
I am interested in furthering the cause of online privacy tools that ordinary people can use.
I am interested in furthering the cause of decentralized alternatives to dominant social media platforms.
I am interested in furthering the cause of legal defense for free speech cases.

implicationNotes: All of these should imply "I care about free speech and open information".
Privacy, surveillance, and decentralized-platform statements should also imply "I care about digital rights and internet freedom".
Censorship-resistant publishing should imply the decentralization meta statement.

groups.title: Open-source software and digital public infrastructure

statements.text: I am interested in furthering the cause of open-source software that serves as public infrastructure.
I am interested in furthering the cause of sustainable funding for maintainers of critical open-source libraries.
I am interested in furthering the cause of decentralized internet infrastructure (IPFS, peer-to-peer protocols, etc.).
I am interested in furthering the cause of open standards that prevent platform lock-in.
I am interested in furthering the cause of open-source security tools.

implicationNotes: All of these should imply "Open-source software deserves sustained funding" and "Open-source public infrastructure benefits everyone".
Decentralized internet infrastructure should also imply "I care about digital rights and internet freedom".

groups.title: Scientific research

statements.text: I am interested in furthering the cause of open-access scientific publishing.
I am interested in furthering the cause of research into diseases that pharmaceutical companies won't prioritize.
I am interested in furthering the cause of independent replication studies.
I am interested in furthering the cause of scientific research free from financial conflicts of interest.
I am interested in furthering the cause of longevity and healthspan research.
I am interested in furthering the cause of research into the roots of the chronic disease epidemic.

implicationNotes: Open-access scientific publishing should imply "Scientific research funded by the public should be freely accessible".
The public-health-oriented research statements should connect to "I care about public health".

groups.title: Public health

statements.text: I am interested in furthering the cause of mental health treatment and research.
I am interested in furthering the cause of evidence-based drug addiction treatment.
I am interested in furthering the cause of independent research into nutrition and preventive medicine.
I am interested in furthering the cause of pandemic preparedness infrastructure outside of government control.
I am interested in furthering the cause of affordable healthcare options.

implicationNotes: All of these should imply "I care about public health".
Pandemic preparedness should also imply "I care about local community resilience".

statements.text: I am interested in furthering the cause of alternatives to the traditional school system.
I am interested in furthering the cause of homeschooling resources and curriculum.
I am interested in furthering the cause of vocational training and trades education.
I am interested in furthering the cause of tutoring and educational support for disadvantaged kids.
I am interested in furthering the cause of research into what actually improves learning outcomes.
I am interested in furthering the cause of keeping classic literature and history in the curriculum.

groups.title: Local community
notes: Intentionally framed to resonate with both left-coded local resilience language and right-coded self-reliance language.

statements.text: I am interested in furthering the cause of local food systems: farmers' markets, community-supported agriculture, local farms.
I am interested in furthering the cause of local journalism and community news.
I am interested in furthering the cause of mutual aid networks in local communities.
I am interested in furthering the cause of community-owned infrastructure.
I am interested in furthering the cause of preserving local culture and history.
I am interested in furthering the cause of community resilience and self-reliance.

implicationNotes: All of these should imply "I care about local community resilience".
Local food systems should connect to the hidden-majority local-food commonality statement.

groups.title: Environment and sustainability

statements.text: I am interested in furthering the cause of research into clean energy technologies.
I am interested in furthering the cause of independent environmental monitoring.
I am interested in furthering the cause of conservation of local natural areas.
I am interested in furthering the cause of sustainable agriculture research.
I am interested in furthering the cause of reducing dependence on industrial food supply chains.

implicationNotes: All of these should imply "I care about environmental stewardship".
Clean-energy and sustainable-agriculture statements should also imply "I care about local community resilience".

groups.title: Faith, civil society, and charitable coordination

statements.text: I am interested in furthering the cause of cross-church coordination on shared community projects.
I am interested in furthering the cause of religious freedom.
I am interested in furthering the cause of faith-based charitable work.
I am interested in furthering the cause of civil society organizations as alternatives to government programs.
I am interested in furthering the cause of tools that help communities coordinate without bureaucracy.

implicationNotes: Civil-society alternatives should imply "Local problems are best solved by local people with local knowledge".

groups.title: Crypto and decentralized finance
notes: Likely early-user audience for Commonality and closely related to the system's thesis.

statements.text: I am interested in furthering the cause of crypto education and adoption.
I am interested in furthering the cause of decentralized finance tools accessible to ordinary people.
I am interested in furthering the cause of privacy-preserving financial tools.
I am interested in furthering the cause of financial self-sovereignty.
I am interested in furthering the cause of crypto as a path to economic freedom.

implicationNotes: Privacy-preserving finance should imply "I care about digital rights and internet freedom".
Financial self-sovereignty should imply the permissionless-funding meta statement.

groups.title: Defense, security, and resilience (d/acc)

statements.text: I am interested in furthering the cause of defensive cybersecurity research and tools.
I am interested in furthering the cause of decentralized infrastructure that is hard to shut down.
I am interested in furthering the cause of biosecurity threat research and early warning systems.
I am interested in furthering the cause of supply chain resilience.
I am interested in furthering the cause of individual and community preparedness.

implicationNotes: Hard-to-shut-down infrastructure should imply "I care about digital rights and internet freedom".
Biosecurity and preparedness statements should connect to "I care about public health".

groups.title: Left-coded causes
notes: These balance a likely initial right-leaning user base without becoming fake centrist filler.

statements.text: I am interested in furthering the cause of economic opportunity for people left behind by globalization.
I am interested in furthering the cause of affordable housing in high-cost cities.
I am interested in furthering the cause of workers' ability to organize and negotiate without requiring traditional unions.
I am interested in furthering the cause of reducing incarceration for nonviolent offenses.
I am interested in furthering the cause of independent oversight of police departments.
I am interested in furthering the cause of legal aid for people who can't afford a lawyer.

implicationNotes: Independent police oversight should connect to the police-accountability hidden-majority commonality statement.
Reducing incarceration for nonviolent offenses should connect to the criminal-justice commonality statement.

groups.title: The funding infrastructure itself
notes: Statements about Commonality and the broader public-goods-funding problem; useful for bootstrapping the project's own community.

statements.text: I am interested in furthering the cause of building better infrastructure for funding public goods.
I am interested in furthering the cause of reducing overhead in charitable giving.
I am interested in furthering the cause of transparency and accountability in how donated money is spent.
I am interested in furthering the cause of decentralized alternatives to traditional philanthropy.
I am interested in furthering the cause of tools that let people coordinate collective action without needing a central organization.

implicationNotes: All of these should imply "I care about improving our ability to fund public goods".
All of these should imply the collective-funding technology statement, the public-goods-underfunded statement, and the permissionless-funding statement.
